parsed_review.summary: CP00-727 (RP24 Korean Legal Depth P02 Permission Fixture Bridge, risk B, 40 units, RP24.P02.M04.S19-RP24.P02.M06.S14) passes hardened read-only review with no findings. The pack is correctly in pre-review state (manifest/construction-inspection/adjudication/command-evidence/claude-review-result all report production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, status pending_review); it does not falsely assert a production_ready, runtime, or enterprise-trust claim — the production_ready transition is gated on exactly one valid receipt, which this review provides. Implementation (registry.js, service.js, validators.js, index.js) is strictly descriptor-only: KOREAN_LEGAL_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION and per-row ROW_EXTRAS hold writes_product_state/creates_database_rows/external_runtime_called/hwpx_runtime_opened/court_filing_runtime_opened/litigation_deadline_runtime_opened/corporate_document_runtime_opened/korean_clause_final_authority_opened/permission_decision_detail_included/audit_event_body_included/cross_tenant_access_allowed all false, with synthetic_only/no_real_data true. validateDescriptor enforces these guards plus claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, and local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false. CP727 deliverable distribution (test:8,contract:2,implementation:17,security_audit:4,ui:5,claude_review:2,failure_recovery:2=40) and micro_title distribution (Secondary Workflow Slice:4, Permission And Audit Binding:22, Synthetic Fixture Set:14) match the baseline exactly, confirmed by recount of included_units. Tests cover CP727 binding/coverage, descriptor runtime-closed assertions, and contract projection (latest_pack=CP00-727, cp722-726 historical, validation.valid=true); the full validation ladder in command-evidence.json (closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check, plus node --check and rp24 gates) all exit 0. Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic descriptor evidence packets (emits_runtime_receipt=false, writes_product_state=false, receipt_shape descriptor_only_korean_legal_no_write) and the contract-generation script — not product runtime (hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false). Claude remains read-only (Read,Grep,Glob) and non-final approver; readiness auth preflight passed with valid JSON (exit 0, 1228 bytes), valid_review_receipts and invalid_review_attempts are empty, and no auth-failure/0-byte/malformed/fenced/tool-call-shaped output is being accepted as valid evidence. No real client/matter/credential/secret data present. Dirty files outside active scope (closeout-pack-plan.json, next-pack-queue.json, progress-control-room, workbook/*, ldip-integration invalid-attempt) are correctly classified unrelated per changed-file-scope.json and not treated as part of this pack. blocks_goal_closeout=false reflects this CP closeout only; the human/enterprise-trust authority boundary is expected and outside this receipt.
